screenshots.figma: Figma node 785:33970 screenshot captured via MCP get_screenshot

compositionAnalysis.overallShape: PASS - Dark charcoal section with rounded-3xl pseudo-element background matches Figma. Section padding (py-10 sm:py-16) creates appropriate vertical breathing room.
compositionAnalysis.majorElements: PASS - All major elements present and correctly positioned: ThemeHeading (2-column with eyebrow, heading, description, secondary button), controls row (progress bar + circular navigation arrows), 3-column card carousel.
compositionAnalysis.relativeSizes: PASS - Heading area proportional to card area. Cards equally sized in 3-column grid at desktop. Controls row is compact (~40-48px tall) matching Figma.
compositionAnalysis.visualWeight: PASS - Dark background with white text creates appropriate contrast. Accent-colored progress bar and card elements provide visual interest matching Figma.
compositionAnalysis.overflow: PASS - No content overflow or clipping observed at any breakpoint. Long heading variation wraps properly.
compositionAnalysis.interactiveElements: PASS - Navigation arrows visible and accessible. Progress bar visible. Secondary button in ThemeHeading visible on the full-content variation.

observations: 2-column ThemeHeading grid (md:grid-cols-2 gap-x-20) correctly splits eyebrow+heading left and description+button right at desktop, stacking on mobile
Controls row (flex items-center gap-4 mb-6) places progress bar and nav arrows on a single row between heading and cards
Swiper shows 3 slides at desktop (>=900px container), 2 at tablet (>=570px), 1 at mobile -- matches spec responsive breakpoints
spaceBetween=24 creates consistent card gaps matching Figma's visual spacing
Dark pseudo-element (before:bg-charcoal before:rounded-3xl before:-inset-(--side-gutter) md:before:-inset-8) creates the rounded dark background panel
ThemeHeading margin (mb-8 md:mb-16) creates appropriate gap between heading area and controls row
Controls row margin (mb-6) separates progress bar/nav from cards
Section padding (py-10 sm:py-16) matches expected vertical rhythm
Container pseudo-element inset creates proper edge padding around content
Heading uses text-header-2 (headingSize=2) which renders the Anton font at the correct display size -- matches Figma heading proportions
Eyebrow uses uppercase text-sm font-medium -- matches Figma's small uppercase treatment
Description text appears at body size -- matches Figma right column description
Card headings use text-header-3 for post cards and text-header-5 for resource/policy cards -- appropriate for card context
Section uses dark mode (class='dark bg-transparent!') -- always dark per DEC-001
Background pseudo-element uses bg-charcoal -- matches Figma dark background
Text is white (via dark mode) -- matches Figma
Progress bar track uses dark:bg-white/20 -- subtle track visible in screenshots
Progress bar fill uses accent gradient (--gradient-left to --gradient-right) -- accent green visible in screenshots matching Figma
Navigation buttons use carousel-nav-btn with white border in dark mode -- matches Figma circular arrow buttons
Background pseudo-element uses rounded-3xl -- creates the rounded panel matching Figma
Card images use rounded-xl -- visible rounded corners on card images
Navigation buttons use rounded-full -- circular matching Figma
Progress bar uses rounded-full -- matches Figma thin rounded bar
ThemeHeading left column (eyebrow + heading) is left-aligned -- matches Figma
ThemeHeading right column (description + button) is left-aligned within its column -- matches Figma
Navigation arrows are right-aligned in the controls row (progress bar takes flex-1) -- matches Figma
Cards are evenly distributed across the carousel width
3 equal-width cards at desktop matches Figma's 3-card layout
Card image aspect ratio (281/333 for posts, 395/317 for team members) creates visually appropriate proportions
Navigation buttons are w-10 h-10 / md:w-12 md:h-12 -- appropriately sized circles matching Figma

variationChecks.notes: Heading only, no eyebrow/description/button. ThemeHeading correctly hides when only heading provided. 5 post cards loaded (3 visible at desktop).
All fields populated. 2-column ThemeHeading with eyebrow, heading, description, secondary button all render correctly. Matches Figma layout most closely.
Team member cards render with accent background, photo, department badge, name, role, and CTA. Card design matches the accent-background pattern shown in Figma.
Resource cards render with dark background, audience/type badges, title, and CTA. No images as expected for resource cards.
Policy cards render with dark background, topic badges, title, and CTA. Compact card style appropriate for policy content.
Renders with all blog posts (selectedPosts populated in editor). Same card rendering as automatic mode.
ThemeHeading correctly hidden when no eyebrow/heading/description/buttons provided. Carousel renders directly under container.
Section renders because policy posts exist. hideIfEmpty only hides when query returns zero results -- correct behavior.
Long heading and description wrap properly within their columns. Long button text wraps within button. No overflow observed.

Controls row correctly hidden when both showPagination and showNavigation are false. Only swiper container with cards renders. Confirmed by page audit: progressBar=false, navPrev=false, navNext=false, controlsRow=false.

accessibilityVisualChecks.contrast: PASS - White text on charcoal background provides sufficient contrast. Accent-colored elements (badges, progress bar) have adequate contrast against their backgrounds.
accessibilityVisualChecks.focusIndicators: Note - Focus states on navigation buttons and card links not visually verifiable from static screenshots. CSS uses standard focus patterns from carousel-nav-btn class.
accessibilityVisualChecks.ariaAttributes: PASS - Page audit confirms aria-labelledby attributes on sections linking to heading IDs. Navigation buttons have aria-label='Previous Item'/'Next Item' and title attributes.

notes.observation: Figma shows generic placeholder cards with teal accent background and serif headings. Implementation uses real card-post.php, card-team-member.php, card-resource.php, and card-policy.php templates which have their own established designs. This is intentional per planning decision DEC-004: card accent colors and styling come from post type card template parts, not from this block.

Cannot verify hover states on navigation buttons (gradient sweep animation), card hover states (resource/policy cards have bg-accent hover), or focus indicators from static screenshots. CSS code includes appropriate transition and animation definitions.
